findings.problem: The chapter repeatedly stops to translate its own implications into thesis sentences after the scene has already made the point. Those interpretive glosses sharpen the theme once or twice, but repeated use turns consequence into explanation and slightly bleeds pressure from the grave-opening and pump scenes.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the explanatory glosses at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:147, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:161, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:207, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:335, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:337. Replace at least two of those thesis-forward sentences with concrete, observable consequence in the scene itself: crowd reaction, Lucille's procedural behavior, Rafe's bodily shame, or Della's physical response to the tags. Let the legal and thematic stakes emerge from action and image rather than narrator summary.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised passages anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:147, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:161, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:207, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:335, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:337 end on concrete action, dialogue, or sensory consequence, and no more than one of those beats still uses an abstract sentence to explain what the dispute means. Fail if two or more of those scene turns still rely on narrator interpretation instead of dramatized consequence.

findings.problem: A single sentence architecture recurs often enough to become audible: negative-balance phrasing such as "Not X. Not Y. Just Z." and closely related "not because ... but because ..." turns. It is effective in the coffin scene, but the repeated reuse later in the chapter reads as a prose tic rather than a fresh choice and violates the constitution's ban on recurring formulas appearing more than twice.
findings.rewrite_direction: Keep the strongest instance of this cadence and vary the others at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:121, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:173, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:179, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:325, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:337. Recast those beats into different syntactic shapes grounded in Della's perception, bodily action, or object detail so the prose no longer lands its emphasis through the same repeated negative turn.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised line-clusters anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:121, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:173, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:179, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:325, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:337 contain no more than one remaining "Not X. Not Y." or equivalent negative-balance cadence across the set, with the other beats rewritten into recognizably different sentence shapes. Fail if the same cadence still appears in two or more of those targeted passages.

summary: The chapter lands its causal spine cleanly and meets the chapter-01 obligations: Della is hired for grave knowledge, Sadie's exhumation immediately turns naming into compensation conflict, Lucille recognizes memory as legal leverage, and Rafe's resentment arrives with specific economic pressure. Dialogue and character voice are strong and style-bible-consistent, and the exhumation is rendered with enough bodily specificity to avoid sanitization. The chapter still fails craft/prose pass level because it keeps translating its own stakes into repeated negative-balance formulas and thesis-forward glosses instead of trusting the strongest scene details to carry the weight.
